definition: Grimoire preserves and co-assembles a grammatically intact multi-clause document skeleton (prolog + DOCTYPE/external-subset + element/attribute body, often with specific co-dependent tokens such as a SYSTEM reference plus a name-bearing attribute or a prefixed element) so deep parser validation paths are reached; cmplog corrupts the prolog or stays on one fixed template, short-circuiting the required grammar before these paths are entered.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage and structural mutators reproduce the deep nested DTD wholesale as a generalized token/gap structure, producing identical interned dict pointers (name3==name3) that cmplog's byte-level havoc cannot reconstruct coherently.
signature.one_line: Grimoire rebuilds nested DTD structure to produce identical interned dict pointers

analysis.summary_one_line: Resolving the name3 pointer-identity check requires a deeply-nested DTD that interns and re-looks-up the same name3 string so dict pointers alias.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid, deeply-nested DTD whose name3 string is reproduced verbatim at the right structural position so the dict interns identical pointers; the byte run must be spliced as a coherent token/gap structure, not a random edit.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire's GeneralizationStage abstracts corpus DTD bodies into token/gap structures and its Grimoire mutators splice/recurse them, reproducing the well-formed nested DOCTYPE wholesale (resolved 10/10, hitcount 1004, far above any other fuzzer).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has I2S but only havoc+token byte-level mutation; it cannot reconstruct the deep nested structure as a unit and stays blocked 8/10 with hitcount 2.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural Grimoire mutators (string/gap recombination) rebuild the deep DTD structure that cmplog's byte-level havoc cannot; the grimoire_structural delta is the enabler.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a pointer-identity comparison of name3 inside xmlHashLookup3 on the dict path.
The comparison sits on the dict-interned branch, where equality is pointer identity established by dict interning.
The branch is reached via deep DTD parsing (DOCTYPE / entity / attribute-list-decl call chain).
minimizer beats naive on the calibrated_energy delta, resolving 9/10 vs naive 0/10.
minimizer derives per-seed mutation energy from intrinsic calibration which naive lacks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
the energy uses ONLY intrinsic calibration and is NOT weighted by how often
a region has been hit

evidence_trail.claim: naive runs the havoc+token stack in a plain StdMutationalStage with no I2S and no calibrated energy.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: fast is identical to minimizer except the FAST AFLFast power schedule.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore `fast`
vs `minimizer`, not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: FAST schedule damps saturated paths to 0.4x energy via the n_fuzz factor.
minimizer beats fast on the aflfast_rarity delta (9/10 vs 2/10).
grimoire beats cmplog on the grimoire_structural delta, resolving 10/10 with hitcount 1004.
grimoire adds a GeneralizationStage plus structural Grimoire mutators on top of the cmplog stack.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog uses only havoc+token plus I2S, with no structural recombination.
value_profile_cmplog beats value_profile on the I2S delta (8/10 vs 8 blocked).
I2SRandReplace scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has the CMP_MAP gradient but no I2SRandReplace mutator.
value_profile_cmplog beats plain cmplog on the value_profile delta (8/10 vs 2/10).
The CMP_MAP records operand-distance buckets and preserves inputs that bring operands closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: The winning grimoire seed contains a complete DOCTYPE/SYSTEM dtd reference structure verbatim.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness isolating the name3 dict-interned identity check where value_profile_cmplog and value_profile resolve at equal rates (no I2S advantage), and where grimoire shows no structural advantage over cmplog, would refute the multi-feature attribution.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The I2S hypothesis is unverified in lineage because no vpc winning seed was bisected for this branch, so the I2S-vs-CMP_MAP split between Pairs 4 and 5 rests on mechanism reasoning rather than seed-byte evidence.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's structural splicing assembles the co-occurrence of an external-DTD defaulted attribute and an explicit matching attribute on the same element instance, creating the pointer-equality collision that cmplog's byte-level havoc cannot reach.
signature.one_line: Grimoire assembles DTD-defaulted plus explicit attribute co-occurrence for pointer collision

analysis.summary_one_line: A DTD-defaulted attribute whose interned name+prefix also appears as an explicitly-specified attribute on the same start-tag, triggering the dedup match.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML document with a DTD that declares default attributes (e.g. via <!DOCTYPE ... SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd">) on an element AND an element instance that explicitly specifies one of those same defaulted attributes, so that during attribute-defaulting the defaulted attname/aprefix dict pointers equal an already-stored atts[j]/atts[j+1] pair.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds build the full nested structure: a DOCTYPE referencing an external DTD plus an element with an explicitly-specified attribute (e.g. <a><b f="..."), so a defaulted attribute collides with the explicitly-given one and the true branch is taken (winner hits the branch 18 times vs 0 for the loser).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds carry a syntactically valid DOCTYPE prologue but never assemble the co-occurrence of a DTD-defaulted attribute together with the same attribute explicitly present on a matching element instance; the loser enters the defaulting dedup loop (L9667 false 38 times) but never satisfies the inner pointer-equality so the true side stays at 0.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both cmplog and grimoire run the identical input-to-state comparison-solving stack, so comparison-solving cannot be the divergence cause. The single-technique delta is grimoire structural: its GeneralizationStage plus the Grimoire structural mutators splice and recursively replace generalized token/gap structures to assemble the deep, multi-clause XML+DTD nesting required to make a defaulted attribute coincide with an explicit one — recombination cmplog's byte/havoc mutation cannot reach.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true branch is the attribute-defaulting dedup test comparing a defaulted attname/aprefix against already-stored atts[j]/atts[j+1] pointers.
The branch sits inside the DTD attribute-defaulting block which iterates over declared default attributes.
Winner grimoire seeds contain a DOCTYPE referencing an external DTD plus an element with an explicitly specified attribute.
The winner seed includes an element with an explicitly given attribute (f=).
The winner resolves the branch in all 10 trials and hits it 18 times while the loser blocks 8/10 and hits it 0 times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=18  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The loser enters the defaulting dedup loop but never takes the true side of the blocker condition.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L9668  T=8 F=9  T=0 F=38  if ((attname == atts[j]) && (aprefix == atts[j+1]))  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Both cmplog and grimoire run the I2SRandReplace stage, so I2S is not the technique delta; grimoire adds generalization plus structural mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire's structural mutators splice and recurse on generalized token/gap structures, enabling grammar-free structural recombination.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: A winning seed was produced by a chain of Grimoire recursive replacement mutations.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness requiring a DTD-defaulted attribute that collides with an explicitly-specified attribute in which plain cmplog resolves the gated branch as often as grimoire would refute the claim that grimoire's structural recombination is the divergence cause.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact byte-level mechanism by which the defaulted attribute's interned pointer comes to equal an explicit attribute's pointer is inferred from source structure, not directly observed in a fully-decoded winning document.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural mutators generate structurally varied DOCTYPE/ATTLIST documents that reach xmlAddAttributeDecl on a fresh external subset with empty child list; cmplog byte-edits one fixed template and always enters with an already-populated dtd->last.
signature.one_line: Grimoire reaches first ATTLIST on empty DTD child list; cmplog always pre-populated

analysis.summary_one_line: A structurally valid DTD whose external subset reaches xmlAddAttributeDecl with dtd->last==NULL, i.e. the first declaration linked into a fresh subset.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input that drives the SAX parser through xmlSAX2AttributeDecl into xmlAddAttributeDecl on the external subset (ctxt->inSubset==2) at a point where the DTD's child list is still empty, so the dtd->last==NULL true branch fires (line 2164 -> 2165). This requires assembling a well-formed XML document with a DOCTYPE/external-subset declaration plus a syntactically valid first <!ATTLIST> declaration, including varied element/attribute structure (e.g. xlink attributes, alternate encodings, nested elements) that the cmplog fixed template never produces.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds carry diverse, structurally recombined XML: ISO-8859-5 encoding decls, nested '<a><b xlink="">' elements, varied DOCTYPE/SYSTEM dtds references, and processing-instruction variants. The W role hits xmlCheckDefaultedAttributes (44 vs 0), xmlSAX2StartElement (44 vs 0) and xmlSAX2AttributeInternal (28 vs 0), and reaches line 2165 (dtd->children=dtd->last=ret), confirming the first-declaration true branch executes on the external subset. Branch L2164 shows W T=27 F=9 vs L T=0 F=66 — the winner takes the true side 27 times while the loser never does.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: All five cmplog loser seeds are near-identical fixed boilerplate ('<?xml version="1.0"?>' + '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/127772...') sharing the same 06 00 00 00 prefix and only trivial byte-level mutation (DwordInteresting, ByteDec/ByteAdd, BytesSwap). cmplog's I2S + havoc cannot synthesize the structural variation needed to enter the external subset with an empty child list, so L2164 is taken false 66 times and never true; the loser instead routes through xmlSAX2ElementDecl/xmlAddElementDecl (0 vs 57 in W) and xmlValidateAttributeDecl (0 vs 9), a different declaration-handling path that leaves dtd->last already populated.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage abstracts corpus XML into <GAP> token structures and the Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireExtensionMutator, GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator, GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator) splice/recurse on those grammar-free token structures, generating the well-formed-yet-novel DTD/ATTLIST arrangements that reach xmlAddAttributeDecl on a fresh external subset. cmplog has the same I2S stage but lacks generalization + structural recombination, so it stays stuck on byte-level edits of one template.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true branch of dtd->last==NULL inside xmlAddAttributeDecl, fired when the first declaration is linked into the subset.
Only the winner executes the first-declaration assignment dtd->children=dtd->last=ret at line 2165.
At the blocker the winner takes the true side 27 times while the loser takes it zero times (false 66).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1  L2164  T=27 F=9  T=0 F=66  if (dtd->last == NULL) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is grimoire>cmplog whose single-technique delta is grimoire_structural; grimoire adds generalization + Grimoire mutators on top of cmplog's I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire winner seeds contain structurally recombined XML such as nested elements with xlink attributes.
Grimoire winner seeds carry alternate encoding declarations such as ISO-8859-5.
Loser cmplog seeds are near-identical fixed boilerplate sharing the same 06 00 00 00 prefix and 127772.xml template.
Loser cmplog seeds only receive trivial byte-level havoc mutations, not structural recombination.
The winner exercises element-start and defaulted-attribute SAX paths that the loser never enters.
The loser instead routes through xmlAddElementDecl, a different declaration path not taken by the winner.
Grimoire's GeneralizationStage and structural mutators perform grammar-free structural recombination of token/gap structures.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness in which grimoire and cmplog reach xmlAddAttributeDecl's dtd->last==NULL true branch at statistically indistinguishable rates, showing structural recombination provides no advantage over cmplog's I2S+havoc for assembling the first valid <!ATTLIST> declaration in a fresh external subset.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The seed-provenance note warns the storing fuzzer may differ from the decisive winner/loser, so attributing the winner seeds' structural diversity specifically to grimoire's mutators rests partly on the mutation_op chains rather than a controlled per-fuzzer comparison.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's Extension/RecursiveReplacement/RandomDelete mutators splice grammar-shaped fragments to assemble a valid DOCTYPE+attribute layout that populates the element table for the first time; cmplog's corrupted prolog prevents establishing the fresh-DTD-then-attribute sequence.
signature.one_line: Grimoire assembles fresh DOCTYPE+attribute sequence triggering empty element table creation

analysis.summary_one_line: A structurally valid DOCTYPE with SYSTEM external-DTD plus an attribute declaration that first creates the element table.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed XML prolog followed by `<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/...dtd">` and a first attribute declaration, so xmlAddAttributeDecl calls xmlGetDtdElementDesc2 on a DTD whose element table is still NULL (taking the blocked true side at valid.c:3286 and creating the table).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds carry intact structural tokens — `<?l ?><!DOCTYPEa SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd">` and `<a><b xlink="">` — assembled by Grimoire splice/recurse mutators that preserve grammar-shaped token runs, so the parser reaches the DTD/attribute path and hits the table-creation true branch (W: T=27 F=9 at L3286, avg hitcount 286).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds keep a corrupted prolog region (`06 00 00 00 31 32 37 37 37 32 2e 78 6d 6c` / `=====27772.`) ahead of the `<?xml` token and never establish the fresh-DTD-then-attribute sequence that first populates the element table, so they take the false side (L: T=0 F=66 at L3286) and stay blocked 19.5h on average.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural: the GeneralizationStage abstracts corpus bytes into <GAP> token structures and the Grimoire mutators (Extension/RecursiveReplacement/RandomDelete) splice grammar-shaped fragments, building the valid DOCTYPE+attribute layout that bare cmplog (havoc + token + input-to-state byte substitution, no structural recombination) cannot assemble, even though both fuzzers run the same i2s stage.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side at valid.c:3286 is the element-table-creation path inside xmlGetDtdElementDesc2.
The blocker is reached only via xmlAddAttributeDecl, which calls xmlGetDtdElementDesc2 when processing an attribute declaration.
The decisive single-technique delta is grimoire's generalization plus Grimoire structural mutators, since both fuzzers already run I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire winner seeds contain intact DOCTYPE/SYSTEM/attribute token structure produced by Grimoire structural mutators.
A Grimoire winner seed adds an attribute-bearing element fragment <a><b xlink=""> that drives attribute-declaration processing.
cmplog loser seeds carry a corrupted byte run ahead of the XML prolog and never form the fresh-DTD attribute sequence.
The winner takes the true side and the loser takes the false side at the blocker line.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L3286  T=27 F=9  T=0 F=66  if (dtd->elements == NULL) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire abstracts byte runs into gap structures and splices them, enabling grammar-free structural assembly that cmplog lacks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's mutation set is havoc plus token plus I2SRandReplace byte substitution, with no structural recombination.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where cmplog (havoc + I2S, no structural mutators) reaches the table-creation true side at the same or higher rate than grimoire — showing structural recombination is not what assembles the valid DOCTYPE+attribute layout.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The seed bytes show valid DOCTYPE/SYSTEM/attribute structure in winners but I cannot directly prove the Grimoire mutators (vs the I2S stage both fuzzers share) authored that structure, since grimoire lineage rows are nameless.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's structural mutators build a syntactically valid Name token containing '_' or ':' within a real DOCTYPE/attribute-decl structure; cmplog's havoc produces long single-byte runs that never form a valid Name token feeding xmlIsDocNameChar.
signature.one_line: Grimoire synthesizes valid Name token with '_'/':'; cmplog produces only byte runs

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry a structurally valid XML Name token (e.g. with '_' or ':') reaching attribute-value validation so xmlIsDocNameChar sees a name char.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed XML DTD/attribute construct whose Name token contains a character matched at valid.c:3555 (c == '_' or c == ':'), reached only after the input parses into a real DOCTYPE/attribute-decl structure rather than a flat byte run.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The grimoire winner seed is a structured XML document with a real DOCTYPE declaration (<!DOCTYPEa SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd">); grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural mutators recombine generalised token/gap structures so name-token characters flow into xmlIsDocNameChar, and the True side of L3555 is actually taken (T=60).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser seeds are dominated by long single-byte runs (offset-concentrated 0x37('7')x38 etc.) from havoc/I2S byte substitution; they never synthesize a Name token that satisfies '_'/':' so L3555 stays False (T=0 F=161) and the branch blocks.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural mutation is the winning edge: the GeneralizationStage abstracts corpus entries into token/gap structures and the Grimoire structural mutators (extension/recursive-replacement/string-replacement) splice grammar-free structural fragments, building the syntactically valid Name token that drives the attribute-decl validation path; cmplog's comparison-operand substitution can only swap logged CMP bytes at matching offsets and cannot construct the surrounding grammatical structure, so it cannot present a valid Name char to this gate.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the True side of the name-char test c == '_' || c == ':' inside xmlIsDocNameChar.
The decisive divergence is grimoire over cmplog with the grimoire_structural technique delta.
The winner-resolving seed is a structured XML document containing a real DOCTYPE declaration.
At the blocker line the winner takes the True branch 60 times while the loser never does.
The loser cmplog seeds are dominated by long single-byte runs characteristic of byte-level mutation rather than structured grammar.
Grimoire adds a GeneralizationStage plus structural mutators on top of the full cmplog/I2S stack, and the single-technique delta versus cmplog is generalization + Grimoire mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
it includes the `CmpLogObserver`, the `TracingStage`, and the
`I2SRandReplace` (i2s) stage — and ADDS a `GeneralizationStage` plus
Grimoire structural mutators.

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire mutators splice and recurse on generalised token/gap structures via string-based grammar-free structural mutation.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where cmplog reaches the True side of xmlIsDocNameChar at a rate statistically indistinguishable from grimoire once a valid Name token with '_'/':' is present, indicating I2S substitution alone (not structural generalization) suffices to satisfy the gate.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The inference that grimoire's structural generalization (rather than its identical I2S stage or scheduling luck) is the specific cause rests on seed-shape contrast rather than direct lineage attribution, since grimoire and cmplog share the I2S stack and grimoire lineage is nameless.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural mutators assemble nested DOCTYPE+element markup (preserving prolog, SYSTEM reference, and prefixed attribute) that routes attribute value parsing into xmlIsDocNameChar; cmplog seeds carry leading garbage that malforms the document earlier.
signature.one_line: Grimoire preserves nested DOCTYPE+element grammar reaching attribute name char validation

analysis.summary_one_line: A well-formed DTD-bearing XML doc whose attribute value reaches the old-XML-1.0 name-char check at valid.c:3573 with a letter/digit char

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid XML document carrying a DOCTYPE with a SYSTEM-referenced DTD plus element/attribute markup (e.g. <!DOCTYPE...SYSTEM "dtds/...dtd">...<a><b xlink:f="">), so an attribute declaration's value is parsed and routed into xmlValidateNmtokens/Name/AttributeValueInternal, calling xmlIsDocNameChar with a real name character on the doc->properties XML_DOC_OLD10 path.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds are short, well-formed XML where a clean <?l ?> declaration, <!DOCTYPEa SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd"> and concrete element/attribute markup <a><b xlink:f=""> reach the validation call chain; the generalization+structural-recombination stage assembles and preserves this nested grammar structure so the attribute value feeds xmlIsDocNameChar with the true branch taken (W shows L3573 T=123).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds carry a leading filename/garbage prefix (e.g. 127772.xml\n or 06 00 06 00 00 00 31 32 37 37 37 32 ...) before the <?xml version="1.0"?> declaration, so the document is malformed earlier and the attribute value path that reaches the true side fires far less often (L shows L3573 T=30); havoc/I2S byte substitution does not reliably build the nested DOCTYPE+element grammar that grimoire structural mutation does.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders and the Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator, GrimoireExtensionMutator) splice/recurse on those generalized token structures, enabling grammar-free assembly of the nested DOCTYPE+element document that cmplog (I2S byte substitution only) cannot synthesize.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where cmplog (no grimoire structural stage, no CMP_MAP) resolves the old-XML-1.0 name-char true branch as often as grimoire/value_profile_cmplog, showing structural recombination and gradient feedback give no advantage at building the nested DOCTYPE+element document.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Whether the XML_DOC_OLD10 else-path requirement is genuinely the discriminator versus simple document well-formedness is inferred from source plus seed shape, not directly proven by a per-role branch hit on line 3547.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage and structural mutators splice DTD/element token/gap structures, rapidly assembling inputs where an attribute-value head is a non-NameChar — a structural recombination cmplog's byte-level mutations cannot produce.
signature.one_line: Grimoire structural splicing assembles non-NameChar-leading attribute value

analysis.summary_one_line: Reach an ATTLIST NMTOKENS/ENUMERATION attribute whose validated value begins with a non-NameChar, so xmlIsDocNameChar fails and the function returns early.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same NMTOKENS/ENUMERATION attribute context, but reached by structurally recombining generalised DTD/element token fragments so the attribute value begins with a non-NameChar; grimoire reaches it far faster (avg blocked 0.10h, hitcount 333) than cmplog.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire winner seeds carry Grimoire structural mutators (mutation_op=GrimoireExtensionMutator, GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator, GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator) and are compact restructured DTD inputs; grimoire resolves 10/10 with avg duration blocked 0.10h and hitcount 333, indicating it rapidly assembles the valid DTD+ATTLIST skeleton and varies the attribute-value head structurally.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser stays blocked 8/10 at avg duration 19.10h with hitcount 2; it has the cmplog I2S stage but lacks the GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators, so it cannot recombine the DTD token structure to land a non-NameChar leading attribute value within budget.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire builds on the full cmplog stack (so I2S is held constant between grimoire and cmplog) and ADDS a GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural mutators that splice generalised token/gap structures; this grammar-free structural recombination of the DTD/element skeleton is the single-technique delta (grimoire_structural) that produces the qualifying attribute value.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the !xmlIsDocNameChar test in xmlValidateNmtokensValueInternal and the globally blocked side is the true branch.
The function is reached only for NMTOKENS or ENUMERATION attribute types via the caller switch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
case XML_ATTRIBUTE_NMTOKENS:
[B]  3875  	case XML_ATTRIBUTE_ENUMERATION:

evidence_trail.claim: Winners take the true side (early return 0); losers fall into the acceptance loop.

value_profile resolves 8/10 against naive's 2/10 with the value_profile delta.
The value_profile technique adds a CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback that preserves inputs whose operands get closer to a target.

An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose attribute-value first-character rejection is gated by a single equality/character-class CMP that value_profile resolves no faster than naive (no per-execution CMP_MAP distance-bucket progress), so value_profile shows no resolution advantage over naive at this branch.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Whether the value_profile advantage comes from a true CMP_MAP distance gradient over xmlIsDocNameChar's character-class comparisons rather than from incidental faster corpus growth is inferred from mechanism text, not directly observed at the CMP level.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's structural recombination assembles a syntactically valid multi-token (space-containing) attribute value inside a non-CDATA DTD attribute declaration, reaching the interior-space branch; cmplog reproduces parse structure and DTD literals but never constructs an attribute value with an interior space.
signature.one_line: Grimoire assembles interior-space attribute value in non-CDATA DTD attribute

analysis.summary_one_line: A DTD-declared non-CDATA attribute whose value contains an interior 0x20 space after non-space content, so normalization enters the space-run branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed XML document declaring an element with a non-CDATA (tokenized) attribute via DOCTYPE/DTD, where an attribute instance carries a value containing a 0x20 space *interior* to the value (a space after at least one non-space character) so xmlValidNormalizeString's while(*src!=0) loop reaches a space and takes the if(*src==0x20) true branch at valid.c:2607.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The grimoire winning seed is a structurally complete XML doc (`<?xml...><!DOCTYPE...SYSTEM "dtds/..."><a><b xlink:hr'f=...`) reaching the normalizer 14 times and crossing the blocker (W: L2607 T=3), with grimoire's generalization/structural mutators able to splice space-separated attribute tokens into a valid DTD-validated attribute value, satisfying the non-CDATA + interior-space requirement.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocked on all 10 trials (loser blocked=10, avg hitcount on branch=0); its seeds reach the normalizer (L: L2606 T=135) but never the interior-space branch (L: L2607 T=0 F=135) — its havoc/I2S mutations reproduce the parse structure and even the same DTD literals but never produce an attribute value with an internal space inside a non-CDATA attribute, so every entry into the loop is a non-space character routed through the else arm.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage abstracts byte runs into <GAP> placeholders and the Grimoire structural mutators (extension/recursive/string-replacement) recombine these generalized token structures grammar-free, letting it construct a syntactically valid multi-token (space-containing) attribute value in a DTD-tokenized attribute; plain cmplog (havoc+token+I2S) lacks this structural recombination and cannot assemble the required interior-space attribute, so the divergence is attributable to grimoire structural mutation.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the interior-space test inside the normalization loop, true side globally blocked.
Entering the true branch starts a run-of-spaces skip then emits a single normalized space, the winner-only code.
Normalization is invoked from attribute-value processing on non-CDATA attributes (CDATA returns early).
The grimoire winning seed is a structurally complete XML document with DOCTYPE/DTD and attribute markup.
The winner crosses the blocker true side while the loser never does despite reaching the loop.
The loser cmplog stays blocked on all trials with zero hits on the branch.
grimoire adds a GeneralizationStage and structural mutators on top of the full cmplog stack including I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
it includes the `CmpLogObserver`, the `TracingStage`, and the
`I2SRandReplace` (i2s) stage — and ADDS a `GeneralizationStage` plus
Grimoire structural mutators

evidence_trail.claim: The Grimoire mutators perform grammar-free string-based structural recombination of generalized token/gap structures.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness requiring an interior-space non-CDATA attribute value in which plain cmplog reaches the if(*src==0x20) true branch at the same rate as grimoire, showing structural recombination is not what produces the multi-token attribute value.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: I infer the winner contains an interior-space non-CDATA attribute value from structural completeness of the seed, but the shown 64 byte window does not directly display the space-containing attribute value that triggers the true branch.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage and structural mutators co-assemble a multi-construct skeleton (XML prolog + DOCTYPE external reference + body with undeclared element name) that resolves to XML_ELEMENT_TYPE_UNDEFINED in mixed-content checking; cmplog's I2S fixes scalar comparands but cannot build this multi-construct DTD+body structure.
signature.one_line: Grimoire assembles DTD+body skeleton placing undeclared element in XML_ELEMENT_TYPE_UNDEFINED case

analysis.summary_one_line: A structurally valid XML doc with a DOCTYPE/DTD that references an element name having no matching element declaration (etype UNDEFINED).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed XML input deep enough to reach mixed-content checking inside parsed element content while a DTD/DOCTYPE is loaded, where the current element node's name resolves through xmlGetDtdElementDesc to an elemDecl whose etype is XML_ELEMENT_TYPE_UNDEFINED (a referenced but never-declared element). This requires a multi-construct skeleton: a valid XML prolog, a <!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd"> external-subset reference, plus a body with character data inside an element whose name has no <!ELEMENT> declaration.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winning seeds carry a complete, structurally coherent skeleton: prolog (<?m.?>), <!DOCTYPEa SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd">, and a body <b>...; grimoire's generalization+structural recombination splices these token/gap structures so the parser descends through xmlParseCharData -> areBlanks -> xmlIsMixedElement with myDoc and node->name set, and the named element resolves to an UNDEFINED elemDecl, taking the true branch (W marks 3497/3498 and L3499 false; d=1 L3497 shows W T=26 F=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog seeds reach the surrounding parser machinery but never satisfy the mixed-content path to the UNDEFINED case: at the blocker d=1 L3497 cmplog (L) shows T=0 F=30, and at the call site d=2 areBlanks L2921 'if (ret == 0) return(1)' the loser takes T=30 (ELEMENT_TYPE_ELEMENT, a declared element) while never producing an undeclared-element reference; cmplog's I2S byte substitution fixes scalar comparands but cannot assemble the multi-construct DTD+body skeleton that places an undeclared element name in content position.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both cmplog and grimoire run the same shared input-to-state comparison-solving stage, so that technique is NOT the delta between them. The grimoire-only delta is the GeneralizationStage (replacing concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders) plus the Grimoire structural mutators that splice and recurse on generalised token/gap structures (string-based, grammar-free structural mutation). This grimoire structural recombination assembles the deep, multi-construct well-formed XML+DTD skeleton that reaches the mixed-element check with an undeclared element name, which the shared scalar comparison-solving stage alone (as in cmplog) cannot build.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is grimoire>cmplog with the single-technique delta grimoire_structural, and both fuzzers share the I2S stage.
The grimoire/cmplog delta is structural, not I2S, because grimoire builds on the full cmplog I2S stack.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire adds a GeneralizationStage and structural mutators that splice generalised token/gap structures.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker requires elemDecl->etype to be XML_ELEMENT_TYPE_UNDEFINED, the true branch of the switch.
At the blocker the winner takes the UNDEFINED true side (T=26) while the loser never does (F=30) and instead takes the declared-ELEMENT case.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L3497  T=26 F=0  T=0 F=30  case XML_ELEMENT_TYPE_UNDEFINED:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winning grimoire seeds contain a complete XML prolog plus a DOCTYPE SYSTEM DTD reference and a body element.
Loser cmplog seeds reach the parser but, at the areBlanks call site, resolve to a declared element (ret==0 true) rather than producing an undeclared-element reference.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=2   L2921  T=0 F=26  T=30 F=0  if (ret == 0) return(1);

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness in which cmplog reaches the XML_ELEMENT_TYPE_UNDEFINED true branch as often as grimoire — i.e., if scalar I2S byte substitution alone, without grimoire's structural recombination, suffices to assemble the well-formed XML+DTD skeleton placing an undeclared element name in content position, then the structural-recombination attribution is wrong.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The winning seeds' recorded mutation_op names the shared comparison-solving stage, not a Grimoire mutator, so the attribution to grimoire's structural recombination rests on the technique delta and the deep multi-construct skeleton rather than a direct lineage trace of a Grimoire mutator producing the find.
